methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $20M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries, all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $20M raised as a baseline proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage step-ups, implies a $40M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $400M, with a range spanning $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI). The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in scale and capability maturity within the Defense & Security frontier-tech segment.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $34M (floor) to $460M (ceiling), with a median of $220M. The gap between stage-progression ($40M) and peer-median ($400M) reflects uncertainty around whether Rune's technology, team, or market position align with higher-performing peers or the lower end of the Defense & Security comp set. Key uncertainties include the repeatability of the company's technical differentiation, customer concentration risk, and timing to defensible revenue scale in a capital-intensive sector.
